steps.explanation: Extract the key fact. One serving (\(x = 1\)) provides 180 mg of potassium, which equals 5% of the daily allowance. So one serving contributes \(p = 5\) percentage points.
Scale linearly. Each serving independently contributes 5 percentage points. For \(x\) servings: \(p = 5 \cdot x = 5x\). This is a linear (proportional) relationship.
Verify with \(x = 1\): \(p = 5(1) = 5\). Check: 1 serving = 5% of daily allowance. Correct.

Evaluate each choice.
  • Choice A: \(p = 0.5x\) — gives \(p = 0.5\) when \(x = 1\), but one serving provides 5%, not 0.5%. Eliminate A.
  • Choice B: \(p = 5x\) — gives \(p = 5\) when \(x = 1\), matching the given fact that one serving provides 5% of daily allowance. This is correct.
  • Choice C: \(p = (0.05)^x\) — an exponential expression; at \(x = 1\) gives \(p = 0.05\), which is 0.05%, not 5%. Eliminate C.
  • Choice D: \(p = (1.05)^x\) — an exponential growth expression; at \(x = 1\) gives \(p = 1.05\), not 5. Eliminate D.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
